lean_translation:
def lcm (a b : Nat) : Nat :=
  if a = 0 || b = 0 then 0 else (a / Nat.gcd a b) * b

theorems.name: lcm_comm
theorems.statement: theorem lcm_comm (a b : Nat) : lcm a b = lcm b a

theorems.name: lcm_zero_left
theorems.statement: theorem lcm_zero_left (b : Nat) : lcm 0 b = 0

theorems.name: lcm_dvd_left
theorems.statement: theorem lcm_dvd_left (a b : Nat) : a ∣ lcm a b

lean_translation:
def power (base exp : Nat) : Nat :=
  match exp with
  | 0 => 1
  | e + 1 => base * power base e

theorems.name: power_zero_exp
theorems.statement: theorem power_zero_exp (b : Nat) : power b 0 = 1

theorems.name: power_one_exp
theorems.statement: theorem power_one_exp (b : Nat) : power b 1 = b

theorems.name: power_eq_pow
theorems.statement: theorem power_eq_pow (b e : Nat) : power b e = b ^ e

lean_translation:
def is_prime (n : Nat) : Bool :=
  if n < 2 then false
  else if n = 2 then true
  else if n % 2 = 0 then false
  else Id.run do
    let mut i := 3
    while i * i <= n do
      if n % i = 0 then return false
      i := i + 2
    return true
lean_tests:
#eval is_prime 2  -- true
#eval is_prime 7  -- true
#eval is_prime 10  -- false

theorems.name: is_prime_implies_gt_one
theorems.statement: theorem is_prime_implies_gt_one (n : Nat) : is_prime n = true → n > 1

theorems.name: not_is_prime_zero
theorems.statement: theorem not_is_prime_zero : is_prime 0 = false

theorems.name: not_is_prime_one
theorems.statement: theorem not_is_prime_one : is_prime 1 = false

lean_translation:
def mod_exp (base exp mod : Nat) : Nat :=
  if mod = 0 then 0 else (base ^ exp) % mod

theorems.name: mod_exp_eq_pow_mod
theorems.statement: theorem mod_exp_eq_pow_mod (b e m : Nat) : m > 0 → mod_exp b e m = (b ^ e) % m

theorems.name: mod_exp_lt_mod
theorems.statement: theorem mod_exp_lt_mod (b e m : Nat) : m > 0 → mod_exp b e m < m

theorems.name: mod_exp_zero_exp
theorems.statement: theorem mod_exp_zero_exp (b m : Nat) : m > 0 → mod_exp b 0 m = 1 % m

theorems.name: sum_digits_zero
theorems.statement: theorem sum_digits_zero : sum_digits 0 = 0

theorems.name: sum_digits_le
theorems.statement: theorem sum_digits_le (n : Nat) : sum_digits n ≤ n

theorems.name: sum_digits_mod_nine
theorems.statement: theorem sum_digits_mod_nine (n : Nat) : sum_digits n % 9 = n % 9

theorems.name: count_digits_pos
theorems.statement: theorem count_digits_pos (n : Nat) : count_digits n ≥ 1

theorems.name: count_digits_zero
theorems.statement: theorem count_digits_zero : count_digits 0 = 1

theorems.name: count_digits_single
theorems.statement: theorem count_digits_single (n : Nat) : n > 0 → n < 10 → count_digits n = 1

tests:
def gcd(a, b):
    while b != 0: a, b = b, a % b
    return a
assert gcd(48, 18) == 6
assert gcd(101, 103) == 1
assert gcd(0, 5) == 5
assert gcd(7, 0) == 7

theorems.name: gcd_comm
theorems.statement: theorem gcd_comm (a b : Nat) : gcd a b = gcd b a

theorems.name: gcd_zero_right
theorems.statement: theorem gcd_zero_right (a : Nat) : gcd a 0 = a

theorems.name: gcd_self
theorems.statement: theorem gcd_self (a : Nat) : gcd a a = a

theorems.name: gcd_dvd_left
theorems.statement: theorem gcd_dvd_left (a b : Nat) : gcd a b ∣ a

lean_translation:
def lcm (a b : Nat) : Nat :=
  if a = 0 || b = 0 then 0 else (a / Nat.gcd a b) * b
tests:
def gcd(a, b):
    while b != 0: a, b = b, a % b
    return a
def lcm(a, b):
    if a == 0 or b == 0: return 0
    return (a // gcd(a, b)) * b
assert lcm(12, 18) == 36
assert lcm(0, 5) == 0
assert lcm(7, 1) == 7

theorems.name: lcm_comm
theorems.statement: theorem lcm_comm (a b : Nat) : lcm a b = lcm b a

theorems.name: lcm_zero_left
theorems.statement: theorem lcm_zero_left (b : Nat) : lcm 0 b = 0

theorems.name: lcm_dvd_left
theorems.statement: theorem lcm_dvd_left (a b : Nat) : a ∣ lcm a b

lean_translation:
def power (base exp : Nat) : Nat :=
  match exp with
  | 0 => 1
  | e + 1 => base * power base e
tests:
def power(base, exp):
    result = 1
    while exp > 0:
        if exp % 2 == 1: result *= base
        base *= base
        exp //= 2
    return result
assert power(2, 10) == 1024
assert power(3, 4) == 81
assert power(5, 0) == 1

theorems.name: power_zero_exp
theorems.statement: theorem power_zero_exp (b : Nat) : power b 0 = 1

theorems.name: power_one_exp
theorems.statement: theorem power_one_exp (b : Nat) : power b 1 = b

theorems.name: power_eq_pow
theorems.statement: theorem power_eq_pow (b e : Nat) : power b e = b ^ e

source:
def is_prime(n: int) -> bool:
    if n < 2: return False
    if n == 2: return True
    if n % 2 == 0: return False
    i = 3
    while i * i <= n:
        if n % i == 0: return False
        i += 2
    return True
lean_translation:
def is_prime (n : Nat) : Bool :=
  if n < 2 then false
  else if n = 2 then true
  else if n % 2 = 0 then false
  else Id.run do
    let mut i := 3
    while i * i <= n do
      if n % i = 0 then return false
      i := i + 2
    return true
tests:
def is_prime(n):
    if n < 2: return False
    if n == 2: return True
    if n % 2 == 0: return False
    i = 3
    while i * i <= n:
        if n % i == 0: return False
        i += 2
    return True
assert is_prime(2)
assert is_prime(7)
assert not is_prime(10)
assert not is_prime(1)
lean_tests:
#eval is_prime 2  -- true
#eval is_prime 7  -- true
#eval is_prime 10  -- false

theorems.name: is_prime_implies_gt_one
theorems.statement: theorem is_prime_implies_gt_one (n : Nat) : is_prime n = true → n > 1

theorems.name: not_is_prime_zero
theorems.statement: theorem not_is_prime_zero : is_prime 0 = false

theorems.name: not_is_prime_one
theorems.statement: theorem not_is_prime_one : is_prime 1 = false

source:
def mod_exp(base: int, exp: int, mod: int) -> int:
    if mod == 0: return 0
    result = 1
    base = base % mod
    while exp > 0:
        if exp % 2 == 1: result = (result * base) % mod
        exp //= 2
        base = (base * base) % mod
    return result
lean_translation:
def mod_exp (base exp mod : Nat) : Nat :=
  if mod = 0 then 0 else (base ^ exp) % mod
tests:
def mod_exp(base, exp, mod):
    if mod == 0: return 0
    result = 1
    base = base % mod
    while exp > 0:
        if exp % 2 == 1: result = (result * base) % mod
        exp //= 2
        base = (base * base) % mod
    return result
assert mod_exp(2, 10, 1000) == 24
assert mod_exp(5, 3, 13) == 8

theorems.name: mod_exp_eq_pow_mod
theorems.statement: theorem mod_exp_eq_pow_mod (b e m : Nat) : m > 0 → mod_exp b e m = (b ^ e) % m

theorems.name: mod_exp_lt_mod
theorems.statement: theorem mod_exp_lt_mod (b e m : Nat) : m > 0 → mod_exp b e m < m

theorems.name: mod_exp_zero_exp
theorems.statement: theorem mod_exp_zero_exp (b m : Nat) : m > 0 → mod_exp b 0 m = 1 % m

tests:
def sum_digits(n):
    s = 0
    while n > 0: s += n % 10; n //= 10
    return s
assert sum_digits(123) == 6
assert sum_digits(0) == 0
assert sum_digits(9999) == 36

theorems.statement: theorem sum_digits_mod_nine (n : Nat) : sum_digits n % 9 = n % 9

source:
def count_digits(n: int) -> int:
    if n == 0: return 1
    c = 0
    while n > 0:
        c += 1
        n //= 10
    return c
tests:
def count_digits(n):
    if n == 0: return 1
    c = 0
    while n > 0: c += 1; n //= 10
    return c
assert count_digits(123) == 3
assert count_digits(0) == 1
assert count_digits(9) == 1

theorems.name: count_digits_pos

theorems.statement: theorem count_digits_zero : count_digits 0 = 1

theorems.name: count_digits_single
theorems.statement: theorem count_digits_single (n : Nat) : n > 0 → n < 10 → count_digits n = 1
